zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Randall Jr.於1659年3月6日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克卡培 波爾波時，他的父親Richard Randall Sr.52歲，母親Elizabeth Irons17歲。他於1693年與Elizabeth Tozier在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾達勒姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1729年6月16日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Randall Jr.于1659年3月6日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克卡培 波爾波时，他的父亲Richard Randall Sr.52岁，母亲Elizabeth Irons17岁。他于1693年与Elizabeth Tozier在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾達勒姆结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1729年6月16日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Randall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randell
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German   Randel  .

variants.name: Randon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel.
English: probably a variant of Randall , with assimilation of n-l to n-n.
French (northern): from the ancient Germanic personal name Rando, a short form of names based on the element rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’.

variants.name: Randol
meanings: English: altered form of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randal
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Randal in Gulen, a compound of Old Norse runnr ‘copse, thicket, grove’ + dalr ‘valley’.

variants.name: Randles
meanings: English and Irish (Louth): variant of Randall , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland this is sometimes a variant of Reynolds .

variants.name: Randle
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German Randel or of its Bavarian variant Randl.

variants.name: Rendell
meanings: English (Somerset, Devon, and Dorset): variant of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randel
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
South German: variant of   Rand   3.

variants.name: Randolph
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rendall
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Scottish: habitational name from Rendall (Orkney), named with a stream-name of uncertain origin + Old Norse dalr ‘valley’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Swedish   Rendahl  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Randall Jr. was born on 6 March 1659, in Cape Porpoise, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Richard Randall Sr., was 52 and his mother, Elizabeth Irons, was 17. He married Elizabeth Tozier in 1693, in Durham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 16 June 1729, in Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Randall Jr. est né le 6 mars 1659, à Cape Porpoise, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Richard Randall Sr., avait 52 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Irons, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Tozier en 1693, à Durham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 16 juin 1729, à Douvres, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Randall Jr. am 6. März 1659 in Cape Porpoise, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Randall Sr. 52 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Irons 17. Er hat 1693 in Durham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America Elizabeth Tozier geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. Juni 1729 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Randall Jr. è nato il il 6 marzo 1659 nel Cape Porpoise, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Richard Randall Sr., aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Irons, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Tozier nel 1693, nel Durham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 giugno 1729, nel Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Randall Jr.が1659年3月6日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヨークカペ ポルプオイゼで生まれた時，父Richard Randall Sr.は52歳，母Elizabeth Ironsは17歳であった。1693年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアダルハムでElizabeth Tozierと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1729年6月16日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアドバーで70歳で死去した。

ko.Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, Colonia: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Randall Jr.(이)가 1659년 3월 6일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 캡 포르포이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Randall Sr.(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Irons(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1693년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 뒤람에서 Elizabeth Tozier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1729년 6월 16일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 도버에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Randall Jr. nasceu em 6 de março de 1659, em Cape Porpoise, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Richard Randall Sr., tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Irons, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Tozier em 1693, em Durham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 16 de junho de 1729, em Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Randall Jr. родился 6 марта 1659 г. в местности кап порпоис, ерк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Richard Randall Sr. было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Irons, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Tozier в 1693 г. в местности дурам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 16 июня 1729 г. в местности довир, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Randall Jr. nació el 6 de marzo de 1659, en Cape Porpoise, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Richard Randall Sr., tenía 52 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Irons, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Tozier en 1693, en Durham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de junio de 1729, en Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años.
